details.objective:
Build a functioning C compiler from scratch in Python. The compiler should take C source code as input and produce x86-64 assembly output.

Implement the standard compiler pipeline:
1. Lexer/Tokenizer - tokenize C source into tokens
2. Preprocessor - handle #include, #define, #ifdef etc.
3. Parser - parse tokens into an AST (support C89/C90 subset at minimum)
4. Semantic analysis - type checking, symbol table management
5. IR generation - produce an intermediate representation
6. Code generation - emit x86-64 assembly from the IR

Start with a minimal subset of C (integers, basic arithmetic, functions, if/else, while/for loops, pointers, arrays, structs) and expand from there. Each phase should have comprehensive tests.

The compiler should be invocable as: python -m compiler input.c -o output.s

Use .venv/bin/python for all Python commands.

details.title: AST node definitions
Token definitions and Lexer
IR definitions
Recursive descent parser

details.title: Width-correct codegen for short, long, and char types
Add goto/labels to parser, semantic, and IR gen
Assembler and linker integration for executable output
Preprocessor predefined macros and include path search

details.objective:
Continue building and improving the C compiler. Focus on closing known gaps and increasing correctness.

Priority areas:
1. Add long, short, signed type modifiers
2. Add variadic function support (...)
3. Add goto/labels
4. Add bitfields in structs
5. Reduce unnecessary stack spills in register allocator
6. Add #include path search to preprocessor
7. Add _Bool / stdbool.h support
8. Improve codegen quality -- reduce redundant moves and unnecessary temporaries
9. Add more edge-case tests for existing features
10. Fix any bugs discovered by expanding test coverage

Use PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python for all Python commands.

details.title: Add -I include path CLI flag
